0.original: Five or six years ago, a very capable professor told me of his plans for writing a book, 2 00:00:05,600 --> 00:00:10,289 a biography of a controversial personality of a few decades ago. 3 00:00:10,720 --> 00:00:15,169 His ideas were more than interesting, they were alive, fascinating. 4 00:00:15,360 --> 00:00:20,610 The professor knew what he wanted to say, and he had the skill and energy to say it. 5 00:00:20,800 --> 00:00:27,250 The project was destined to reward him with much inner satisfaction, prestige, and money. 6 00:00:28,320 --> 00:00:35,010 Last spring I saw my friend again, and I innocently asked him whether the book was about finished. 7 00:00:35,200 --> 00:00:36,450 This was a blunder. 8 00:00:36,560 --> 00:00:38,450 It opened up an old wound. 9 00:00:38,880 --> 00:00:40,929 No, he hadn't written the book. 10 00:00:41,120 --> 00:00:46,929 He struggled with himself for a moment, as if he were debating with himself whether to explain why. 11 00:00:47,280 --> 00:00:50,289 Finally, he explained that he had been too busy. 12 00:00:50,480 --> 00:00:54,610 He had more responsibilities and just couldn't get to it. 13 00:00:55,360 --> 00:01:01,730 In reality, what the professor had done was to bury the idea deep in his mental graveyard. 14 00:01:02,000 --> 00:01:04,850 He let his mind grow negative thoughts. 15 00:01:05,120 --> 00:01:10,289 He visualized the tremendous work and sacrifices that would be involved. 16 00:01:10,560 --> 00:01:14,289 He saw all sorts of reasons why the project would fail. 17 00:01:15,280 --> 00:01:18,770 Ideas are important, let's make no mistake about that. 18 00:01:19,040 --> 00:01:22,929 We must have ideas to create and improve anything. 19 00:01:23,280 --> 00:01:26,608 Success shuns the man who lacks ideas. 20 00:01:27,120 --> 00:01:30,049 But let's make no mistakes about this point either. 21 00:01:30,240 --> 00:01:33,089 Ideas in themselves are not enough. 22 00:01:33,440 --> 00:01:41,329 That idea for getting more business, for simplifying work procedures, is of value only when it is acted upon. 23 00:01:42,480 --> 00:01:48,609 Every day, thousands of people bury good ideas because they are afraid to act on them. 24 00:01:48,799 --> 00:01:53,170 And afterward, the ghosts of those ideas come back to haunt them. 25 00:01:53,760 --> 00:01:56,450 Put these two thoughts deep in your mind. 26 00:01:56,559 --> 00:02:00,289 First, give your ideas value by acting on them. 27 00:02:00,559 --> 00:02:05,970 Regardless of how good the idea, unless you do something with it, you gain nothing. 28 00:02:07,039 --> 00:02:11,809 Second, act on your ideas and gain mind tranquility. 29 00:02:12,319 --> 00:02:18,530 Someone once said that the saddest words of tongue or pen are these, it might have been. 30 00:02:19,200 --> 00:02:24,770 Every day you hear someone saying something like, had I gone into business seven years ago, 31 00:02:24,880 --> 00:02:27,170 I'd sure be sitting pretty now. 32 00:02:27,600 --> 00:02:30,770 Or, I had a hunch it would work out like that. 33 00:02:31,039 --> 00:02:33,329 I wish I had done something about it. 34 00:02:33,760 --> 00:02:39,250 A good idea, if not acted upon, produces terrible psychological pain. 35 00:02:39,440 --> 00:02:44,289 But a good idea acted upon brings enormous mental satisfaction. 36 00:02:44,720 --> 00:02:46,210 Got a good idea? 37 00:02:46,319 --> 00:02:48,289 Then do something about it. 38 00:02:49,520 --> 00:02:53,569 Use action to cure fear and gain confidence. 39 00:02:53,840 --> 00:02:55,410 Here's something to remember.
0.b1: Five or six years ago, a very skilled professor told me he planned to write a book about a famous person from the past.
0.cn: 五六年前，一位非常有能力的教授告诉我，他计划写一本书，讲述几十年前一位充满争议的人物。
0.ai_notes:
'capable': having the ability or skill to do something well.
'controversial': causing a lot of public disagreement or argument.
'a few decades ago': a period of several ten-year blocks in the past.
'alive': in this context, it means exciting, energetic, or full of potential.
'destined': certain to happen in the future, as if by fate.
'blunder': a careless or stupid mistake.
'opened up an old wound': to remind someone of a painful past experience that they had tried to forget.
'get to it': to find the time or opportunity to start or finish a task.
'mental graveyard': a metaphorical place where someone abandons their forgotten or failed ideas.
'make no mistake': a phrase used to emphasize that something is definitely true.
'shuns': avoids or stays away from something or someone.
'acted upon': to take action based on an idea or suggestion.
'ghosts of those ideas': a metaphor for the regret or guilt one feels about not pursuing a past idea.
'haunt': to cause someone to feel sad or worried for a long time.
'mind tranquility': a state of being calm and peaceful in one's thoughts.
'it might have been': a famous expression of regret for missed opportunities.
'sitting pretty': to be in a very good or comfortable situation, usually financially.
'hunch': a feeling or guess based on intuition rather than known facts.
0.remarks:
'capable': 有能力或技巧做好某事。
'controversial': 引起公众大量分歧或争论的。
'a few decades ago': 过去几十年的时间。
'alive': 在此语境下，指令人兴奋、充满活力或潜力。
'destined': 注定在未来发生，如同命中注定。
'blunder': 粗心或愚蠢的错误。
'opened up an old wound': 提醒某人想起他们试图忘记的痛苦往事。
'get to it': 找到时间或机会开始或完成某项任务。
'mental graveyard': 比喻一个人遗弃被遗忘或失败想法的地方。
'make no mistake': 用于强调某事绝对真实。
'shuns': 避开或远离某人或某事。
'acted upon': 根据想法或建议采取行动。
'ghosts of those ideas': 比喻因未追求过去的想法而感到的遗憾或内疚。
'haunt': 使某人长时间感到悲伤或担忧。
'mind tranquility': 思维平静安宁的状态。
'it might have been': 表达错失良机的著名遗憾短语。
'sitting pretty': 处于非常优越或舒适的境地，通常指经济上。
'hunch': 基于直觉而非已知事实的感觉或猜测。
